title: SoFi Q1 2026 Earnings: Strong Growth Across the Board
text: SoFi Technologies delivered impressive first-quarter results, demonstrating robust expansion in its fintech operations. The company achieved record net revenue of $1.1 billion, representing a 43% year-over-year increase, while net income more than doubled to $166.7 million. The membership base grew substantially to 14.7 million members, up 35% annually, with 1.055 million new members added during the quarter alone. Product offerings expanded 39% to reach 22.2 million total products. Loan originations hit a record $12.2 billion—a 68% jump from the prior year. This growth was driven by personal loans ($8.3 billion, up 51%), student loans ($2.6 billion, up 119%), and home loans ($1.2 billion, up 137%). Net interest income climbed 39% to $693 million, while fee-based revenue increased 23% to $386.8 million. Total deposits grew by $2.7 billion to reach $40.2 billion. CEO Anthony Noto remarked that the health of our consumer base remains strong and noted strong demand continuing into the second quarter. For 2026, SoFi projects adjusted net revenue of approximately $4.655 billion and adjusted EBITDA near $1.6 billion, with anticipated member growth of at least 30% throughout the year.
